verses.text: Este salmo es un himno de alabanza a Dios, el Redentor. Tras una introducción que exhorta a los redimidos a alabar a Dios por su bondad ( **107:1-3** ), el salmista presenta cuatro casos de estudio de personas a quienes el Señor redimió de la adversidad ( **107:4-32** ). Entonces, resume la soberanía del Señor ( **107:33-42** ) y concluye con una invitación a discernir el amor inquebrantable de Dios en todas las áreas de la vida ( **107:43** ).\n\n
Los redimidos podrían ser todos aquellos que salieron del exilio.
"los ha congregado": los exilados, personas que vinieron de todas las naciones a donde el pueblo de Israel y Judá había sido dispersado.
Aquellos que se perdieron en el desierto agradecen a Dios por haberlos rescatado. El desierto podría ser una metáfora de apartarse del camino de la sabiduría ( **1:1,** **Pr 4:10-15** ).\n\n
Aquellos que se perdieron en el desierto agradecen a Dios por haberlos rescatado. El desierto podría ser una metáfora de apartarse del camino de la sabiduría ( **1:1,** **Pr 4:10-15** ).\n\n
El salmista no especifica la ocasión de su problema y angustia. La naturaleza general de la adversidad permite a los lectores en diversas circunstancias identificarse con los lamentos.
Dios los guió como un pastor (ver **23:1** ). • El versículo 8 es un estribillo que se repite en **107:15** , **21** y **31** .
Dios los guió como un pastor (ver **23:1** ). • El versículo 8 es un estribillo que se repite en **107:15** , **21** y **31** .

107:9 Such as sit in darkness, and in the shadow of death,.... This is the second instance of persons in distress calling on the name of the Lord; and who, being delivered, are under obligation to praise him, such as are captives and prisoners. The Targum applies it to the Israelites in the Babylonish captivity; but it is much better to interpret it of prisoners in common; whose prisons are generally dark cells or dungeons, and where they are alone, and deprived of the company of the living;
El salmista solicita acción de gracias y alabanza de las personas que están desesperadas por las consecuencias de su rebelión.\n\n
El consejo de Dios abarca su instrucción y su voluntad.

Agotados por su arduo trabajo ( **Lm 5:13** ), las personas cayeron. Aunque esto podría esperarse de los malvados ( **Sal 9:3,** **27:2** ), no fue como la salida de los israelitas de Egipto ( **105:37** ).

107:13 O that men would praise the Lord for his goodness,.... Or, "confess to the Lord his goodness or grace", or "mercy" (g); own and acknowledge it, in delivering them from such a state of darkness and death, of thraldom and captivity; see Rom 6:17. And for his wonderful works to the children of men! or, "confess" them before them; relate and declare them to them, what wonderful things he has done for them; that they may be affected with them, and that they may praise his name together;

107:14 For he hath broken the gates of brass,.... The prison doors made of brass, as sometimes of iron, for the security of the prisoners; see Acts 12:10. And cut the bars of iron in sunder; with which they were barred and secured. Hyperbolical phrases these, as Kimchi, expressing how exceeding strong the prison doors were, and the impossibility of an escape out of them, unless the Lord had delivered them; but when he works, none can let; all obstructions are easily removed by him;

107:15 Their soul abhorreth all manner of meat,.... Not only bread and common food, but dainty meat, the most delicious fare, Job 33:20, in which they most delighted in time of health, and too much indulged themselves in; and by that means brought diseases upon them, which caused this loathing in them, as is common. Thus to those who are distempered with sin, whose taste is not changed, nor can it discern perverse things, the word of God, the Gospel of Christ, which is delicious food, is not relished by them;

107:16 Then they cry unto the Lord in their trouble,.... A time of affliction is a time of trouble, and a proper season for prayer; and by it persons are brought to the throne of grace, when humbled under the mighty hand of God, to seek for relief. Hezekiah in his affliction prayed to the Lord, though Asa sought to the physicians only, and not to the Lord; this is to be understood of such who are convinced of their folly, brought to a sense of sin and danger, and therefore cry to the Lord for his sparing mercy, and pardoning grace.
Los insensatos que recibieron lo que merecían, pero fueron rescatados de la muerte, ofrecen agradecimiento al Señor.\n\n
Los insensatos que recibieron lo que merecían, pero fueron rescatados de la muerte, ofrecen agradecimiento al Señor.\n\n
Los insensatos que recibieron lo que merecían, pero fueron rescatados de la muerte, ofrecen agradecimiento al Señor.\n\n
El Señor crea ( **Gn 1:3** ) y sostiene el universo ( **Hb 1:3** ) simplemente con su palabra ( **Sal 147:18** ); cuando habla, las personas también son sanadas ( **Mt 8:8** ).

107:21 These see the works of the Lord,.... In creation, the sea itself, its flux and reflux; the creatures in it, fishes of various forms and sizes: and in providence, in preserving ships and men in the most imminent danger, and even to a miracle; sometimes causing the wind to change or to subside in a moment, whereby deliverance is wrought. And his wonders in the deep; the strange and wonderful creatures that are in the deep waters of the sea, and to be seen nowhere else;

107:22 For he commandeth, and raiseth the stormy wind,.... Winds are not raised by men, nor by devils, nor by angels, but by the Lord himself; who has created them, holds them in his fist, brings them out of his treasures, and sends them forth to do his will; if he speaks the word, gives but the order, a storm arises at once, and executes what he pleases, Ps 148:8. Which lifteth up the waves thereof; that is, the waves of the sea;
Los marineros agradecen al Señor cuando los rescata del caos y el peligro del mar.\n\n
Los marineros agradecen al Señor cuando los rescata del caos y el peligro del mar.\n\n
Los marineros agradecen al Señor cuando los rescata del caos y el peligro del mar.\n\n

107:30 He turneth rivers into a wilderness,.... A country abounding with rivers, as the country round about Sodom and the land of Canaan were, Gen 13:10. Such an one is sometimes, by the just judgment of God, turned into a desert. And the water springs into dry ground: what was like a well watered garden becomes like dry and barren earth, on which nothing grows.

107:31 A fruitful land into barrenness,.... Or, "into saltness" (t); as Sodom and the land adjacent became a salt sea; and the land of Canaan was threatened to become brimstone, salt and burning, like Sodom; in which nothing was sown, and which bore no grass; see Gen 14:3 and so the Targum, "the land of Israel, which brought forth fruit, he hath destroyed, as Sodom was overthrown.'' For the wickedness of them that dwell therein; this was the cause of the overthrow of Sodom, and of the destruction of that fine country, as also of Canaan afterwards;
Este llamado a la alabanza pública amplía los refranes en **107:21-22** y **107:31** .
El Señor puede optar por hacer la tierra fértil o estéril ( **74:15,** **Is 35:6-7** ).
El Señor puede optar por hacer la tierra fértil o estéril ( **74:15,** **Is 35:6-7** ).
El Señor puede optar por hacer la tierra fértil o estéril ( **74:15,** **Is 35:6-7** ).
Estas dos secciones ( **107:33-38** , **39-42** ) profundizan en el tema de la soberanía del Señor sobre aspectos que los humanos no pueden controlar.\n\n
Estas dos secciones ( **107:33-38** , **39-42** ) profundizan en el tema de la soberanía del Señor sobre aspectos que los humanos no pueden controlar.\n\n
La presencia de Dios se manifiesta en la abundancia de las familias y los rebaños de su pueblo ( **127:3,** **133:3** ; cp. **109:8** ).
Las personas a menudo se empobrecen cuando sus gobernantes las oprimen.
En su desprecio por los gobernantes humanos, el Señor corrige su corrupción en la tierra.
Las familias numerosas de los pobres son una evidencia de la bendición de Dios.
La justicia prevalece en el mundo de Dios ( **1:6** ), y la historia de Israel demuestra el amor constante de Dios.
